cards.back: A method used in image restoration to reduce noise by selectively smoothing or sharpening areas of the image based on the gradient of the pixel values.

cards.front: What is the effect of Gaussian blur on an image?
cards.back: It reduces high-frequency components, which can help remove noise but also blur fine details.

cards.front: What is the purpose of Wiener filter?
cards.back: To remove noise from an image by estimating the original image based on the measurements of the noisy image and its prior knowledge.

cards.front: What is the difference between mean and median filtering?
cards.back: Median filtering reduces salt and pepper noise more effectively than mean filtering because it is less affected by outliers.

cards.front: What is the role of regularization in image restoration?
cards.back: It helps to prevent the restoration process from over-smoothing the image and preserve important features.
